elements.sent-2411-001: For $f\in \mathcal{S}$, the class univalent functions in the unit disk $\mathbb{D}$ and given by $f(z)=z+\sum_{n=2 ^{\infty a_n z^n$ for $z\in \mathbb{D}$, we improve previous bounds for the second and third Hankel determinants in case when either $a_2=0,$ or $a_3=0$.}}
elements.sent-2411-002: We also improve an upper bound for the coefficient difference $|a_4|-|a_3|$ when $f\in \mathcal{S}$.
elements.sent-2411-003: As usual, let $\mathcal{A}$ be the class of functions $f$ which are analytic in the open unit disc $\D=\{z:|z|<1\}$ of the form \be f(z)=z+a_2z^2+a_3z^3+\cdots, \ee and let $\mathcal{S}$ be the subclass of $\mathcal{A}$ consisting of functions that are univalent in $\D$.
elements.sent-2411-004: In recent years a great deal of attention has been given to finding upper bounds for the modulus of the second and third Hankel determinants $H_2(2)$ and $H_3(1)$, defined as follows who's elements are the coefficients of $f\in\mathcal{S}$ (see e.g. \cite{DTV-book ).}
elements.sent-2411-005: For $f\in\mathcal{S}$ \be H_2(2) = a_2a_4-a_3^2 \end{equation and \begin{equation}\label{eq 3} H_3(1) = a_3(a_2a_4-a_3^2) - a_4(a_4-a_2a_3)+a_5(a_3-a_2^2).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Almost all results have concentrated on finding bounds for $|H_2(2)|$ and $|H_3(1)|$ for subclasses of $\mathcal{S}$, and only recently has a significant bound been found for the whole class $\mathcal{S}$ and for both, $|H_2(2)|$ and $|H_3(1)|$ (see \cite{OT-2021 and \cite{OT-2024 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-007: However finding exact sharp bounds remains an open problem.
elements.sent-2411-008: In their paper \cite{OTT the authors gave the next results concerning coefficients bound and Hankel determinants $|H_2(2)|$ and $|H_3(1)|$.}
elements.sent-2411-009: The estimate given in the case (iii) in the theorem above is the correct one.
elements.sent-2411-010: Similar results are given in \cite{OTT for the case when $a_3=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Since the Keobe function has coefficients $a_n=n$, it is natural to conjecture that $||a_{n+1 |-|a_n||\le1$.}
elements.sent-2411-012: As early as 1933, this was shown to be false even when $n=2,$ when Fekete and Szeg\"o \cite{FekSze33 obtained the sharp bounds \begin{equation}-1 \leq |a_3| - |a_2| \leq \frac{3}{4} + e^{-\lambda_0}(2e^{-\lambda_0}-1) = 1.029\ldots,\end{equation} where $\lambda_0$ is the unique value of $\lambda$ in $0 < \lambda <1$, satisfying the equation $4\lambda =e^{\lambda}$.}
elements.sent-2411-013: Hayman \cite{Hay63 showed that if $f \in {\mathcal S}$, then $| |a_{n+1 | - |a_n| | \leq C$, where $C$ is an absolute constant.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: The exact value of $C$ is unknown, the best estimate to date being $C=3.61\ldots$ (see Grinspan \cite{Gri76 ), which because of the sharp estimate above when $n=2$, cannot be reduced to $1$.}
